message:
fix: stop refusing current-process mailbox ownership for `(deleted)` exes; honour MOCK_AM_BINARY in legacy detect; honour TMPDIR in listener_pid_hint_path; correct percent_decode_path_segment test expectation

Four small, targeted fixes that share no common theme but each
unblock a flaky test or a real-world failure mode.

mcp-agent-mail-db/src/pool.rs (classify_mailbox_ownership)
  Removed the early-return branch that refused to grant
  ownership to the current process when
  `pid_executable_deleted(current_pid)` returned true.  That
  state is normal after:
    - A live binary upgrade (the on-disk binary was replaced
      while the process was still running).
    - `cargo test` rebuilds during a test session (the test
      binary is rebuilt between a probe and the actual run, so
      `/proc/<self>/exe` resolves to a `(deleted)` path even
      though the process is alive and well).
    - Container layer refresh + persistent-volume pid hold-over.
  In all three cases the current process is still alive, still
  running the code it loaded at start, and has full authority
  over its own mailbox.  The "ghost executable" concern only
  applies to OTHER PIDs, which the next branch
  (`competing.first() && executable_deleted`) still handles
  correctly.  Replaced the early-return with a load-bearing
  comment explaining why we no longer fire on the current
  process.

mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/legacy.rs (find_installed_am_binary +
  detect_env_marker tests)
  Added a `MOCK_AM_BINARY` env-var override at the very top of
  `find_installed_am_binary()`: when set non-empty, returns it
  directly; when set to the empty string, returns None.  The
  override lets tests force a known-good or known-missing
  binary path through `with_process_env_overrides_for_test`
  without poisoning the real PATH.  The two previously-failing
  test cases (`pyproject_marker_detected_when_legacy_installed`
  and `legacy_env_defaults_marker_detected`) now wrap their
  body in `with_process_env_overrides_for_test(&[("MOCK_AM_BINARY",
  "")], || { … })` so neither case probes the system PATH for
  a real `am` binary that may or may not be installed on the
  CI runner.  Removed the temporary `panic!()` debug
  instrumentation introduced in the prior commit; the cause
  was the system PATH probe and is now properly mocked.

mcp-agent-mail-server/src/startup_checks.rs (listener_pid_hint_path)
  Read the listener-pid-hint base directory from
  `mcp_agent_mail_core::config::process_env_value("TMPDIR")`
  (with `std::env::temp_dir()` fallback) instead of going
  straight to `std::env::temp_dir()`.  Same rationale as the
  legacy.rs MOCK_AM_BINARY change: routes the path through the
  process-env override machinery so concurrent test runs
  (especially the symlink-safety regression suite) can isolate
  their pid-hint directory from the shared system /tmp.

mcp-agent-mail-server/src/mail_ui.rs
  - `percent_decode_path_segment_keeps_plus_literal` test: the
    expected output for `"topic+a%2Bb"` was previously
    `"topic+a/b"` which doesn't match the function's contract
    (`+` stays literal in path segments, `%2B` decodes to `+`).
    Both occurrences land on the same byte, so the correct
    expectation is `"topic+a+b"`.  Updated the assertion and
    added a comment block explaining the path-vs-query-string
    `+` semantics so the next reader doesn't "fix" it back.
  - `parse_overseer_body` array decoding now applies
    `str::trim` between `as_str` and the `is_empty` filter so
    the no-empty-string guard catches whitespace-only payloads
    too.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
